retry_reason: Failure occurs either because no matching session exists or the refcount was zero (object dying). In both cases, retrying the same lookup without any change in state is unlikely to succeed; if the object was dying it is already gone, and if it did not exist the conditions would need to change externally.
contract_summary: This is a get-wrapper that looks up a tp_vars session by destination, session ID, and role. If a matching entry is found and its reference count is non-zero (i.e., the object is still alive), it acquires a new reference via kref_get_unless_zero and returns the object pointer. The caller receives ownership of this reference and must later call a corresponding release function (e.g., batadv_tp_vars_put) to avoid leaks. The lookup is performed under RCU read lock, but the acquired reference pins the object so it remains valid after RCU unlock. Returns NULL if no matching entry exists or if the entry is already being freed (refcount zero); the caller must check the return value before use. The function does not allocate or create sessions, only retrieves an existing one with a reference.

The only non-refcount logic is the list iteration and comparison of attributes (dst, session, role), which are minimal and necessary for the lookup. The returned pointer carries an elevated refcount.
